definition_population.content: Crohn's disease is a chronic inflammatory intestinal disease characterized by alternating flares and remissions, one of the two major forms of inflammatory bowel disease (ICD-10 K50).

sources.quote: the alternation of periods of flares and remissions influenced by a complex pathogenesis in which inflammation plays a key role

epidemiology.content: IBD prevalence exceeds 0.3% in North America, Oceania, and much of Europe; Crohn's-specific prevalence peaks around 320 per 100,000 in Germany and Canada. Overall IBD affects an estimated 2.2 million in Europe and 1.4 million in North America.

sources.quote: the prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease exceeded 0·3% in North America, Oceania, and many countries in Europe
affects 2.2 million people in Europe and 1.4 million people in North America

biology.content: CYLD was the most significant gene in a genetic association study of Crohn's disease across five European referral centers (2,320 CD patients), and functionally, loss of CYLD (a deubiquitinase) permits NF-κB activation exploited by the IBD-associated pathogen adherent-invasive E. coli.

sources.quote: Ten single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) in four genes were significantly associated with CD: CYLD, USP40, APEH and USP3. CYLD was the most significant gene with the intronically located rs12324931 the strongest associated SNP (p(FDR)=1.74e-17, OR=2.24 (1.83 to 2.74)).

treatment.content: Biologics (TNF antagonists, anti-integrin, anti-IL-12/23) are standard for moderate-to-severe CD. Ustekinumab (Stelara, BLA125261, original FDA approval 2009-09-25) is an approved anti-IL-12/23 biologic used in CD.

sources.quote: Data are needed to inform the positioning of biologic therapy in the treatment of moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease, both first line and after previous biologic exposure.

regulatory_trials.content: 1,368 interventional trials registered on ClinicalTrials.gov for Crohn disease — a mature, heavily studied indication.

sources.quote: count_total query for condition=Crohn disease, study_type=INTERVENTIONAL returned total=1368

gaps: No CD4+ T-cell-restricted mechanistic paper for CYLD found in this pass; the strongest evidence is a genetic association study plus a general epithelial/NF-κB-microbial mechanism, not CD4+ T-cell-specific biology.
